goal: add internal trace salience and caution pressure so prior unstable continuations can narrow admissibility without relying on the external governance sidecar
reason: the external metadata/governance wrapper has been separated into a v15 bolt-on track, so v14 should continue developing its native internal control dynamics

ops.name: compute_trace_salience
ops.purpose: score prior phase history by instability, boundary penalty, blocked entry, and cancellation exposure

ops.name: compute_trace_similarity
ops.purpose: compare current component/topology structure to recent high-salience trace structure

ops.name: compute_caution_field
ops.purpose: project salience-weighted similarity back onto the family lattice as a scalar caution value and local caution field

ops.name: trace_salience_evaluation
ops.position: after component extraction and before operator selection
change: compute salience over recent trace entries
compute similarity between current topology/components and prior salient states
penalize candidate operators when they reinforce high-caution structure
reduce local operator preference in regions with strong caution_field
if caution_scalar exceeds threshold, reduce writeback gain
optionally enter hold_state for one phase instead of committing a risky update
subtract caution_field from corridor window before normalization
reduce entry openness in locally high-caution regions
optionally increase exit resistance under elevated caution

ops.name: component_caution_scores
ops.purpose: aggregate caution_field over component masks so component targeting is caution-aware

cases: high similarity to high-salience prior trace increases caution_scalar
operator ranking changes under strong caution
disabling trace salience restores phase4-like behavior

acceptance_checks: when trace salience is disabled, behavior stays close to phase4
caution localizes to structurally similar regions
component targeting becomes caution-aware without breaking determinism
note: this patch develops native internal ethical restraint inside v14, while the sbmeta sidecar remains an external v15 development/governance wrapper
